IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: MTC WAS FORMED TO CONTRIBUTE TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY, TO PROMOTE THE MODERIZATION OF MO BUSINESSES BY SUPPORTING THE TRANSFER OF SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY AND QUALITY IMPROVEMENT METHODS TO THE WORKPLACE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE MISSION OF THE MTC IS TO HELP STRENGTHEN THE ECONOMY OF MISSOURI THROUGH THE DEVELOPMENT OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY; PROMOTE THE MODERNIZATON OF BUSINESS BY SUPPORTING THE TRANSFER OF SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY AND QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS BY PROVIDING LEADERSHIP IN THE ESTABLISHMENT OF METHODS OF TECHNOLOGY APPLICATION, COMMERCIALIZATION AND DEVELOPMENT.
IRS990.Desc: THE LEWIS AND CLARK DISCOVERY INITIATIVE PROVIDES FUNDS TO THE MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION TO PROVIDE LOANS AND GRANTS TO HIGH-TECH SMALL BUSINESS LOCATED IN MISSOURI.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: THE MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY INVESTMENT PROGRAM FUNDS INNOVATION CENTERS AROUND THE STATE WHOSE PURPOSE IS TO PROVIDE VITAL SERVICES TO TECHNOLOGY STARTUPS OFFERING AN ENVIRONMENT TO FOSTER GROWTH TO A POINT WHERE THESE STARTUPS ARE MORE LIKELY TO RECEIVE VENTURE INVESTMENTS, CORPORATE AND GOVERNMENT CONTRACTS, AND EVENTUALLY ENTER THE MARKETPLACE. THIS FUNDING SOURCE ALSO SUPPORTS MTC'S CAPITAL FORMATION PROGRAM, THE IDEA FUNDS WHICH PROVIDE CO-INVESTMENT TO ELIGIBLE HIGH-TECH, EARLY STAGE AND STARTUP COMPANIES AND ITS MISSOURI BUILDING ENTREPRENEURIAL CAPACITY (MOBEC) PROGRAM WHICH PROVIDES GRANT FUNDING TO REGIONAL AND LOCAL NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS AND HIGHER EDUCATION INSTITUTIONS TO SUPPORT INIAITIVES THAT ASSIST ENTREPRENEURS OR ENHANCES MISSOURI'S RESEARCH AND COMMERCIALIZATION CAPABILITIES.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: THE STATE SMALL BUSINESS CREDIT INITIATIVE (SSBCI) IS A PROGRAM ADMINISTERED BY THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF TREASURY TO SUPPORT STATE PROGRAMS TO INCREASE THE AMOUNT OF PRIVATE CAPITAL MADE AVAILABLE TO SMALL BUSINESSES AND TO COVER REASONABLE ADMINISTRATIVE EXPENSES. MTC USES THE SSBCI PROGRAM TO SUPPORT THE HIGH-TECH MISSOURI INNOVATION, DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURIAL ADVANCEMENT (IDEA) SEED AND VENTURE CAPITAL FUNDS DESIGNED TO HELP HIGH-TECH MISSOURI ENTREPRENEURS TO ACCELERATE PRIVATE INVESTMENT IN MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY EARLY-STAGE COMPANIES AND TO CREATE NEW, HIGH-TECH JOBS IN THE STATE.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: MISCELLANEOUS REALIZED GAIN ON EQUITY INVESTMENTS PROGRAM DEOBLIGATIONS

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO EXPAND THE DIGITAL SANDBOX KC PROGRAM
MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO PROVIDE TRAINING PROGRAMS TO ASSIST ENTREPRENEURS START THEIR BUSINESS WITH HANDS ON LEARNING, MENTORING, & NETWORKING AND FOR AN INNOVATION CENTER IN ST. LOUIS.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO PROVIDE PRODUCTIVITY & COMPETITIVENESS ASSISTANCE TO SMALL & MEDIUM MANUFACTURERS IN MISSOURI.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATION OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN COLUMBIA, MO.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATION OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN KIRKSVILLE, MO TO PROVIDE DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE TO SMALL TECHNOLOGY COMPANIES.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATIONS OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN KANSAS CITY, MISSOURI TO PROVIDE DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE TO SMALL TECHNOLOGY COMPANIES.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATION OF AN INNOVATOIN CENTER IN CAPE GIRARDEAU, MO TO PROVIDE DEVELOPMENT ASSISTANCE TO SMALL TECHNOLOGY COMPANIES.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATIONS OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN JOPLIN, MISSOURI.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE 2018 ANIMAL HEALTH INVESTMENT FORUM SPONSORSHIP.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: MISSOURI UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCES AND TECHNOLOGY

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO CONTINUE EXPANSION OF NSF I-CORPS
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATION OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN SPRINGFIELD, MISSOURI.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF GEOSPATIAL INTELLIGENCE ACCELERATOR RESOURCE CENTER; BUILDING OUT THE AT&T OPEN SOURCE LAB; SUPPORT ENTREPRENEUR & TALENT DEVELOPMENT.

MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE OPERATIONS OF AN INNOVATION CENTER IN ST. JOSEPH, MISSOURI AND FUNDING TO CREATE A PROOF-OF-CONCEPT PROGRAM IN ST. JOSEPH, INNOVATION STOCKYARD, WITH DIGITAL SANDBOX KC,SUPPORT EARLY STAGE COMPANIES THROUGH FUNDING, MENTORSHIP, & INCUBATOR SPACE.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO HELP EXPAND CAPACITY OF ACCELERATOR LABS/PROVIDE STAFFING SUPPORT FOR G2B PROGRAMS, PATENT, MARKET & BUSINESS MODEL EXPLORATION/VALIDATION & TECHNICAL PROOF OF CONCEPTS.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR GROWTH & EXPANSION INITIATIVES.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: DOWNTOWN COUNCIL OF KANSAS CITY MISSOURI

MTC GRANTED FUNDS TO PROVIDE CODING TRAINING IN CAPE GIRARDEAU.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO HELP GROWTH-SEEKING WOMEN ENTREPRENEURS ADVANCE THEIR BUSINESSES IN THE ST. LOUIS REGION.
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR OPERATIONS OF THE OPO STARTUPS PHASE II: INNOVATION DISTRICT

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: ENTREPRENEUR START UP BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: MTC GRANTED FUNDING TO FURTHER DEVELOP, ENHANCE & EXECUTE ITS GLOBAL STARTUP COMPETITION & GROW ST. LOUIS' LEADERSHIP POSITION IN THE NATIONAL & INTERNATIONAL STARTUP LANDSCAPE.,
MTC GRANTED FUNDING FOR THE EXPANSION OF THE HELIX CENTER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION REQUIRES ALL GRANTEES TO PERIODICALLY PROVIDED WRITTEN NARRATIVE AND FINANCIAL REPORTS DESCRIBING USE OF GRANT FUNDING. REPORTS ARE REVIEWED BY MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION STAFF FOR COMPLIANCE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: BOARD MEMBERS OF MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION ARE APPOINTED BY THE GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF MISSOURI.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 WILL BE REVIEWED BY THE AUDIT & FINANCE COMMITTEE AND THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE. A COPY OF FORM 990 WILL ALSO BE PROVIDED TO THE BOARD MEMBERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DURING THE FISCAL PERIOD ENDING JUNE 30, 2010, THE BOARD OF MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION ADOPTED A CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. THE MTC BOARD REVIEWS THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST ANNUALLY AT A BOARD MEETING. BOARD MEMBERS ANNUALLY SIGN ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS STATING THAT THEY HAVE RECEIVED AND REVIEWED THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. ADDITIONALLY, ALL APPLICANTS FOR MTC FUNDING ARE REQUIRED TO DISCLOSE ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE MISSOURI TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION MAKES THESE DOCUMENTS AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990 PART XII FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND REPORTING LINE 1

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION HAS A FINANCIAL COMMITTEE THAT ASSUMES RESPONSIBILTY FOR OVERSIGHT OF THE AUDIT, REVIEW, OR COMPILATION OR ITS FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SELECTIONOF AN INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTANT, THIS PROCESS HAS NOT CHANGED FOR THE CURRENT REPORTING PERIOD.
